title: Psalm xxii. 16, &c. Majesty of His birth and death. Confusion of oracles and demons in Egypt.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Majesty of His birth and death.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Confusion of oracles and demons in Egypt.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or who among those recorded in Scripture was pierced in the hands and feet, or hung at all upon a tree, and was sacrificed on a cross for the salvation of all?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For Abraham died, ending his life on a bed; Isaac and Jacob also died with their feet raised on a bed; Moses and Aaron died on the mountain; David in his house, without being the object of any conspiracy at the hands of the people; true, he was pursued by Saul, but he was preserved unhurt.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Esaias was sawn asunder, but not hung on a tree.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Jeremy was shamefully treated, but did not die under condemnation; Ezechie suffered, not however for the people, but to indicate what was to come upon the people.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Again, these, even where they suffered, were men resembling all in their common nature; but he that is declared in Scripture to suffer on behalf of all is called not merely man, but the Life of all, albeit He was in fact like men in nature.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For “ye shall see,” it says, “your Life hanging before your eyes;” and “who shall declare his generation?”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For one can ascertain the genealogy of all the saints, and declare it from the beginning, and of whom each was born; but the generation of Him that is the Life the Scriptures refer to as not to be declared.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Who then is he of whom the Divine Scriptures say this?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or who is so great that even the prophets predict of him such great things?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: None else, now, is found in the Scriptures but the common Saviour of all, the Word of God, our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For He it is that proceeded from a virgin and appeared as man on the earth, and whose generation after the flesh cannot be declared.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For there is none that can tell His father after the flesh, His body not being of a man, but of a virgin alone; 4. so that no one can declare the corporal generation of the Saviour from a man, in the same way as one can draw up a genealogy of David and of Moses and of all the patriarchs.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For He it is that caused the star also to mark the birth of His body; since it was fit that the Word, coming down from heaven, should have His constellation also from heaven, and it was fitting that the King of Creation when He came forth should be openly recognized by all creation.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Why, He was born in Judæa, and men from Persia came to worship Him.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: He it is that even before His appearing in the body won the victory over His demon adversaries and a triumph over idolatry.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: All heathen at any rate from every region, abjuring their hereditary tradition and the impiety of idols, are now placing their hope in Christ, and enrolling themselves under Him, the like of which you may see with your own eyes.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For at no other time has the impiety of the Egyptians ceased, save when the Lord of all, riding as it were upon a cloud, came down there in the body and brought to nought the delusion of idols, and brought over all to Himself, and through Himself to the Father.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: He it is that was crucified before the sun and all creation as witnesses, and before those who put Him to death: and by His death has salvation come to all, and all creation been ransomed.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: He is the Life of all, and He it is that as a sheep yielded His body to death as a substitute, for the salvation of all, even though the Jews believe it not.
